At the very bottom of the stack, pagination is centered horizontally, with small numbered links and next/previous controls. Overall, the layout feels quiet, structured, and content-first, guiding the eye from the top header through the narrative and into a readable list of offerings without visual noise.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component occupies the top section inside the main white content card, directly below the global header and card’s top padding. It spans the full internal width of the card (about 70–80% of the viewport, centered) with 24–32px of padding above and below. The page title sits at the top-left of this section, in a large red serif heading that runs nearly the full content width but is constrained to 1–2 lines to maintain a strong left-aligned edge. Beneath the title, separated by roughly 16–24px of vertical space, a descriptive paragraph flows left-to-right, using standard body size serif text with comfortable line height and line length (around 60–80 characters). Any inline links or emphasis appear in a contrasting color (e.g., green) but do not break the linear block structure. The overall section height takes up approximately the upper 25–30% of the content card before the interface shifts into more functional elements such as tabs and filters.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the top of the content card; does not scroll with the header and scrolls away normally with page content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The view-toggle tabs are positioned immediately below the page controller section, separated from the description paragraph by about 24px of vertical space. They form a horizontal bar aligned to the left edge of the content card but spanning the full internal width. Typically two main tabs, such as ‘Guided Tours’ and ‘Workshops,’ sit side by side from left to right. Each tab is a flat pill or text label with a subtle rectangular background and 4–8px vertical by 12–16px horizontal padding, maintaining the moderate density. The active tab is emphasized with a red underline or bottom border and stronger text color, while inactive tabs are more subdued but still clearly clickable. The tab row has a thin grey bottom border that visually anchors it and cleanly separates it from the filters below. Overall height of this band is roughly 48–64px including top/bottom padding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal control that remains in place within the flow; switching tabs replaces the filter form, list, and pagination content below without changing its own position.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The guided filters form sits directly beneath the view-toggle tabs, occupying the full width of the content card’s inner area when the ‘Guided Tours’ tab is active. It appears as a low-height horizontal band with 16–24px of top margin from the tabs and 16–24px of bottom margin before the results list. Inside, filter controls (such as date range, language, duration, or audience type) are arranged in a left-aligned row, each control acting like a column in a simplified internal grid: approximately 3–4 filter fields visible per row on a wide screen, each taking about one quarter of the inner width with 8–12px gutters between them. On narrower widths, the fields wrap to a second row while maintaining consistent spacing. Individual inputs are compact rectangles with thin grey borders, 8–10px vertical and 12–16px horizontal padding, and labels set directly above or to the left in smaller serif or unobtrusive sans-serif text. The band itself may sit on a subtly tinted light-grey background or be outlined at the bottom with a fine grey rule to differentiate it from the list that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: shown only when the Guided Tours view is active. It occupies a fixed vertical slot between the tabs and the guided-tours list and scrolls normally with content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This filters form occupies the exact same spatial region as the guided filters form but is displayed only when the ‘Workshops’ tab is active. It also spans the full inner width of the content card and sits 16–24px below the tabs and 16–24px above the workshops list. Filter controls (e.g., skill level, materials included, age group, date) follow the same left-aligned, multi-column pattern: roughly 3–4 fields per row with 8–12px gutters, wrapping to additional rows on smaller viewports while maintaining alignment with the overall content grid. Inputs use the same compact rectangular styling with thin grey borders and conservative padding, ensuring consistent visual rhythm between guided and workshop views. The section’s visual weight matches the guided filters form, reinforcing that these are parallel modes rather than distinct page types.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: shown only when the Workshops view is active, occupying the same vertical slot under the tabs and above the workshops list; scrolls away with content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The guided tours list occupies the main body area beneath the guided filters form when the Guided Tours tab is active. It forms a single vertical column of program cards, aligned to the same left and right padding as the card’s text above. The list begins roughly 16–24px below the filters band and extends downward, often beyond the initial viewport, becoming the primary scrollable region of the page. Each program card spans almost the full inner content width, with 16–24px of vertical spacing between cards, and optionally a thin grey divider line at the top or bottom of each item to reinforce separation. The list region itself does not have a separate background; instead, it continues the white of the content card, preserving a calm editorial feel. Visual emphasis is placed on the topmost few cards, which appear immediately in view after the filters, naturally drawing the eye to the first available tours.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: present only when the Guided Tours view is active. Behaves as a standard scrollable vertical stack within the content card, with no sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The workshops list mirrors the guided tours list in position and structure but is shown only when the Workshops tab is active. It appears directly beneath the workshops filters form, starting about 16–24px below it. Like the guided list, it is a single vertical column of program cards that stretch nearly the full inner width of the content card. Cards are separated by 16–24px of vertical whitespace and may use subtle grey dividers for clarity. The list area carries the same white background as the rest of the card, reinforcing that this is just another mode of the same informational page, not a separate section. As users scroll, this stack occupies most of the vertical real estate, showcasing multiple workshops per screen without feeling cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: shown only when the Workshops view is active. Uses standard vertical scrolling and replaces the guided list in the same spatial slot.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The guided pagination controls sit at the bottom of the guided-tours list, separated from the last program card by approximately 24–32px of vertical space to clearly mark the list’s end. They are horizontally centered within the content card’s inner width, forming a compact, linear strip of page numbers and previous/next arrows. Each pagination link is a small text element or number in serif font, with 4–8px horizontal padding and, optionally, a faint grey outline or simple underline for the current page. Spacing between page numbers remains tight but readable, consistent with the moderate density of the design. The entire pagination band is shallow—roughly 32–48px tall—and is visually understated, acting as a functional terminus rather than a dominant visual element.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: appears only with the guided list. Sits at the very bottom of the guided view stack and scrolls into and out of view like any other block element.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: These pagination controls occupy the same relative position for the workshops list as the guided pagination does for guided tours. They appear 24–32px below the final workshop program card, centered horizontally within the content area. The styling is identical: a row of small serif page numbers and previous/next controls with minimal decoration, thin spacing, and possibly a subtle highlight for the active page. This consistency reinforces the parallel structure of the two views. The band is narrow in height and separated from the card’s bottom padding by an additional 16–24px of space, preventing it from feeling cramped at the edge of the card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally visible: shown only when the Workshops view is active, occupying the same bottom-of-list zone as the guided pagination and scrolling normally with content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart icon navigation sits within the global header bar at the very top of the page, aligned along the top-right utility cluster. Horizontally, it appears to the right of or near the language selector and search icon, maintaining a small 8–16px gap from adjacent elements. Vertically, it is centered within the height of the header, which spans the full viewport width and about 15–20% of viewport height. The icon itself is modest in size (roughly 20–24px square), with sufficient padding around it (8–12px) so it does not appear cramped against the header edges. It uses flat styling in line with the rest of the design, with a simple outline or filled shape, and may include a small numeric badge at its top-right corner to indicate selected programs. Despite being visually accessible, it remains less prominent than the logo and page title, functioning as a practical utility rather than a focal element.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed within the global header region, which remains at the top of the page. The cart icon itself is non-sticky in relation to the content card; as the user scrolls, it remains in the header if the header is fixed, or scrolls away with the header if the header is static (following the site-wide header behavior).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each program card is a horizontally oriented block within the results lists, occupying nearly the full inner width of the content card while respecting 24–32px side padding. The card height is compact but information-rich, typically 120–200px depending on content density. Internally, it follows a simplified 4-column grid: the leftmost 1/4 of the width may house a small rectangular thumbnail image or an icon/date badge, while the remaining 3/4 is reserved for textual details. The text area starts with the program title at the top-left in a slightly larger serif type, often in red to echo the branding. Below the title, separated by 8–12px, sit one or two lines of key metadata (e.g., date, time, duration, language, level) arranged in a single or double row; these may be laid out using subtle separators or muted labels. A short descriptive snippet (1–3 lines) follows, using body text with comfortable line spacing and moderate length, ensuring readability. The card background remains white to blend with the content card, with a thin grey border or a bottom divider line differentiating it from adjacent cards. Vertical padding inside the card is around 16–24px top and bottom, with 16px horizontal padding from the card’s own border to inner content, maintaining the editorial, content-first aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a repeating list item in a vertical stack within either the guided or workshops list. It is non-interfering in layout, expanding vertically with content as needed while maintaining alignment with the overarching grid.

name: Program Detail (Tour or Workshop)
title: Program Detail
description: Displays detailed information and booking options for a single guided tour or workshop, based on the program ID provided in the URL.

core_features: Fetch program details and associated sessions using the provided program ID via getProgramDetail.
Display core program information, including title, description, themes, duration, language, audience, age suitability, location, and program type (guided tour or workshop).
List all available sessions for the program (date, time, currency, price per category, availability) and allow the user to select exactly one session.
Show session availability status based on session capacity and disable or visually mark sessions that cannot be booked.
Collect participant information: for guided tours, counts per category (adult, child, senior); for workshops, participant counts plus an optional group or contact name.
Calculate and display an estimated total price in real time based on selected session prices and participant counts.
Validate required selections and inputs (session selection, participant counts, and required fields by program type) before enabling the primary booking action.
Create a pending ProgramBooking via createProgramBooking and redirect the user to booking_review.html with the resulting booking ID.
Provide navigation back to the tours and workshops listing page using a breadcrumb that routes to tours_workshops.html with the appropriate view parameter.
user_workflows:
View program details and book a session (standard flow):
1. User navigates to program_detail.html?id={program_id} from tours_workshops.html or index.html.
2. On page load, the program ID is read from the URL parameter id.
3. The page calls getProgramDetail with program_id = id.
4. When data is returned, the page displays program information and a list of sessions.
5. The user reviews the program details (description, themes, duration, language, recommended audience, age limits, location).
6. The user selects a specific session from the list (e.g., via radio button or other single-select control).
7. The user enters participant counts (adult_count mandatory; child_count and senior_count optional depending on the program’s pricing model).
8. If the program_type is workshop, the user may optionally enter a group_name (e.g., a family name or school class name).
9. As the user adjusts participant counts, the total price is recalculated using the selected session’s price fields and displayed in the summary panel.
10. Once a valid session and at least one participant are provided, the primary action button ("Book Tour" or "Continue to Review" depending on program_type) becomes enabled.
11. The user clicks the primary action button.
12. The page calls createProgramBooking with program_id, session_id, adult_count, child_count, senior_count, group_name (if provided), and an implementation-defined add_to_cart flag.
13. If createProgramBooking returns success = true, the page redirects to booking_review.html?booking_id={booking.id}.
14. If createProgramBooking returns success = false or fails, an error message is shown and the user can adjust inputs and retry.

Change selected session after entering participants:
1. The user has already selected a session and entered participant counts.
2. The user changes the selected session (e.g., chooses a different date or time from the session selector).
3. The selected ProgramSession object in page state is updated.
4. The price summary recalculates the total price based on the newly selected session’s prices and the existing participant counts.
5. If the new session is not available (capacity constraint) and is selected inadvertently, validation prevents proceeding and shows a relevant message prompting the user to select an available session.

Handle unavailable or fully booked sessions:
1. After getProgramDetail returns, the session list component evaluates each session’s capacity_total and capacity_booked.
2. Sessions that are determined to be unavailable (based on implementation logic that may consider these fields) are marked as unavailable and are not selectable.
3. Available sessions remain selectable. If the user tries to select an unavailable session (e.g., via keyboard navigation), the selection is blocked and an explanatory message is presented.

Navigate back to the tours and workshops listing:
1. The page shows a breadcrumb link labeled with a non-marketing functional label (e.g., "Tours & Workshops").
2. The user clicks the breadcrumb.
3. The page navigates to tours_workshops.html?view=guided, as specified in the navigation rules.

Handle initial load and error states:
1. User requests program_detail.html without a valid id parameter.
2. The page detects that id is missing or empty and displays an error state indicating the program cannot be loaded and provides a link back to tours_workshops.html.
3. If id is present but getProgramDetail fails (network error or not found), the page shows an error message and optionally a retry action that re-invokes getProgramDetail with the same id.
4. During initial data loading, a loading state is displayed while awaiting the getProgramDetail response.
interactions: On page initialization, read URL parameter id and invoke getProgramDetail(program_id = id).
On successful getProgramDetail response, bind the returned program object to the program information component and the sessions array to the session selector component.

When the user clicks a session entry (radio button, list item, or similar), trigger an onChange handler that updates the selected session_id in the page state and recomputes the derived availability and pricing information.

When the user changes participant counts (increment/decrement controls or direct input), trigger an onChange handler that updates adult_count, child_count, and senior_count in page state and recomputes the estimated total price using the selected ProgramSession price fields.
When the user focuses or edits the group_name field (for workshops), trigger an onChange handler to persist the current value into page state.

Before enabling the primary booking button, run validation logic whenever session selection or participant counts change, ensuring that a session is selected and the participant configuration meets minimum requirements defined by the implementation.

When the user clicks the primary booking button, trigger an onClick or onSubmit handler that, if validation passes, calls createProgramBooking with the current program_id, session_id, adult_count, child_count, senior_count, group_name, and an implementation-defined add_to_cart flag.
On successful createProgramBooking response (success = true), trigger navigation to booking_review.html?booking_id={booking.id}.
On unsuccessful createProgramBooking response (success = false) or API error, display a non-blocking error message and keep the current selections so the user can correct issues and retry.
When the user clicks the breadcrumb link, trigger navigation to tours_workshops.html?view=guided.

If the id URL parameter changes while the page is already loaded (e.g., via internal navigation without full reload), clear existing program/session/participant state and re-run getProgramDetail with the new id.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is driven by URL parameters and API responses. The URL parameter id is required and identifies which Program to display: program_detail.html?id={program_id}. On page load (or whenever id changes), the page calls getProgramDetail(program_id = id) and fully replaces the current program and session state with the API response. The selected session, participant counts, and optional group_name are maintained in in-memory page state during the session; they are not persisted to localStorage. The page does not introduce additional required URL parameters beyond id but may be extended to accept optional parameters (for example, a preselected session_id) if needed; such optional parameters would be read on load to initialize the selection, while all canonical data still comes from getProgramDetail. Outgoing navigation passes state forward through URL parameters: on successful booking creation, the page navigates to booking_review.html?booking_id={booking.id}, using the ProgramBooking returned by createProgramBooking. The breadcrumb navigation always routes to tours_workshops.html with view=guided, so the target listing page can adjust its initial view accordingly. No other persistent client-side storage is used; all durable state is either encoded in URL parameters or represented in backend records created via createProgramBooking.

components.functionality: Reads the program ID from the URL, calls getProgramDetail, and exposes the resulting program and sessions data (or loading/error states) to child components.
Displays a breadcrumb trail including a link back to the Tours & Workshops listing and the current program title; handles navigation back to tours_workshops.html with the configured view parameter.

Shows core information for the selected program, including title, type (guided tour or workshop), description, themes, duration_minutes, language, audience_type, min_age, max_age, is_family_friendly, and location.

For each session, determines availability based on capacity_total and capacity_booked (or other implementation rules), exposes an availability status, and prevents selection of sessions deemed unavailable.
Displays an interactive list or grid of sessions for the current program, including date, time, currency, and prices per category; allows the user to select exactly one available ProgramSession.

Collects participant counts and optional group name. Uses the program_type to determine whether to show the group_name field (for workshops) and to adjust labels or helper text. Maintains adult_count, child_count, and senior_count in page state.

Calculates and displays the estimated total price for the current selection using the selected ProgramSession prices and participant counts. The calculation logic multiplies each category’s count by the corresponding price from the selected ProgramSession and sums across categories. It also shows the currency and may display a breakdown by category.

Centralizes validation logic for booking readiness. It listens for changes in selected session, participant counts, and group_name (where applicable) and determines whether the primary booking action should be enabled or disabled, and which validation messages to show.

Handles the primary booking action. When the user initiates booking, it runs final validation; if valid, it calls createProgramBooking with current program_id, selected session_id, adult_count, child_count, senior_count, group_name (if provided), and an implementation-defined add_to_cart flag. It processes the response and triggers navigation to booking_review.html with booking_id on success, or shows error feedback on failure.

Renders the main call-to-action button with a label derived from program_type (for example, a different functional label for guided tours versus workshops). The button’s enabled/disabled state reflects validation from booking-validation-controller and its click event is delegated to booking-submit-controller.

Displays loading indicators while getProgramDetail is in progress, and shows error messages if the program cannot be loaded (missing or invalid id, or API failure). Provides an optional retry control and a link back to the tours and workshops listing.

Coordinates high-level page state, including reading the id URL parameter, reacting to URL changes, resetting state when a new program is loaded, and providing a single source of truth for the selected session, participant counts, and group_name for use by other components.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width grid similar to a 12-column system with a main content column and an internal 4-column image grid. Each image/caption acts like one column of four within the content width.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a strictly linear, top-to-bottom content flow inside a single centered white content card. Above the card, the global red museum header bar remains consistent with the mockup. Inside the card, the order is: small breadcrumb at the very top, then the program-info-panel as a large title with descriptive text, with the session-availability-indicator visually tied to the title. Below this narrative block comes the session-list-selector as a full-width horizontal grid of session cards (mirroring the mockup’s image row). Under that, the participant-selection-form sits as a single-column form block, followed by the price-summary-panel and primary-action-button grouped into a booking footer area. State-related components (loader, error, validation, submit, page-state-manager) appear in context within this vertical flow, either replacing the card entirely (error/loader) or occupying slim bands just above the booking controls (validation/submit states).

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a strictly linear, top-to-bottom content flow inside a single centered white content card. Above the card, the global red museum header bar remains consistent with the mockup. Inside the card, the order is: small breadcrumb at the very top, then the program-info-panel as a large title with descriptive text, with the session-availability-indicator visually tied to the title. Below this narrative block comes the session-list-selector as a full-width horizontal grid of session cards (mirroring the mockup’s image row). Under that, the participant-selection-form sits as a single-column form block, followed by the price-summary-panel and primary-action-button grouped into a booking footer area. State-related components (loader, error, validation, submit, page-state-manager) appear in context within this vertical flow, either replacing the card entirely (error/loader) or occupying slim bands just above the booking controls (validation/submit states).
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header occupies the top 15–20% of the viewport. The main content card is centered and spans roughly 70–80% of the viewport width, with generous light-grey margins on both sides. Within the card, the top 35–40% of the card height is dedicated to the program-info-panel and session-availability-indicator (title and narrative text). The session-list-selector takes the next ~25–35% as a tight, multi-column band. The participant-selection-form, price-summary-panel, and primary-action-button occupy the remaining ~25–30%, forming a visually cohesive booking section. Vertical gaps between these blocks follow the 24–32px rhythm, with internal paddings and gutters in 8–16px increments.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate and text-first. The program-info-panel uses comfortable line lengths and 1.4–1.6 line-height, with 24–32px spacing between heading, subheading, and body copy. The session-list-selector is visually denser—session cards sit close together with 8–12px gutters, maximizing the number of visible sessions per row without feeling cramped. The form fields in the participant-selection-form are arranged to avoid long stretches of empty space while preserving clarity. The price-summary-panel is compact and slightly denser, with smaller vertical gaps between rows to emphasize information efficiency. System messages (validation, errors) are concise and occupy minimal vertical space, preserving the editorial feeling.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye starts on the red global header and logo, then moves right to utilities, before dropping down to the centered card. Within the card, attention flows from the breadcrumb (optional glance) straight to the large red program title, then down through the descriptive text. The session-availability-indicator sits near the title or to its right, drawing a secondary glance without disrupting the reading. From the bottom of the narrative block, the eye moves into the horizontal band of session cards (session-list-selector), scanning left to right across available dates/times. From there, the flow continues down into the participant-selection-form, then to the price-summary-panel where the cost is clarified, and finally lands on the primary-action-button as the culminating focal point. Validation and submission states briefly attract attention near the button but remain visually subordinate to the main content and primary action.

layout.overall_description: The page presents as a calm, editorial-style detail view framed within a centered white card on a light grey background, beneath a strong red museum header bar. At the top of the card, a small, understated breadcrumb line anchors the user within the site’s hierarchy. Below it, a large, traditional serif title in branded red clearly names the tour or workshop, accompanied by a short block of descriptive copy in a dark serif typeface; the overall feeling is that of a museum wall label expanded onto the web. A subtle availability indicator sits adjacent to the title, confirming whether sessions are open without stealing focus from the narrative. As the eye moves downward, the text transitions into a horizontal band of neatly aligned session cards, four to a row within the card’s width, separated by narrow gutters and thin borders reminiscent of archival photo frames. Each session card is compact yet legible, inviting a scan across multiple dates at once. Below this band, the layout returns to a single-column flow: the participant-selection-form stacks clear, labeled fields, followed by a compact price summary block that feels like a small, lightly framed note pinned to the bottom-right of the content. The primary booking button anchors the bottom of the card as a clear call to action in the brand’s red, visually balanced with the title at the top. Loading and error states temporarily replace the card’s inner content with centered, understated messages; validation and submission feedback appear near the booking controls without disrupting the overall vertical rhythm. The entire composition feels like a careful museum placard turned into a functional booking page—serious, readable, and quietly structured around a single column of content with one denser, grid-like band for sessions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The program-detail-loader occupies the exact footprint of the main content card area. When active, the centered white card container still appears in the middle of the viewport beneath the global red header, spanning approximately 70–80% of the viewport width with 24–32px internal padding. Inside this card, all other content is replaced by a vertically centered loading indicator and one or two lines of muted serif placeholder text. The loader sits in the horizontal center of the card, 40–50% down from the top of the viewport, leaving generous whitespace above and below to preserve the calm, editorial feel. The rest of the page (header, background) remains unchanged, ensuring continuity with the overall layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlays the normal card content region during data fetch; anchored within the card and scrolls with the page if the viewport is shorter than the card.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb-navigation is placed at the very top of the white content card, aligned to the left edge of the card’s inner padding. It appears as a single, subtle text line in a small serif or light sans-serif style, using muted grey text with the current page subtly emphasized. It spans only as much width as needed for the breadcrumb labels, typically occupying the top 10–15% of the card’s vertical space. A vertical gap of about 16–24px separates the breadcrumb from the program-info-panel title below, maintaining a clear hierarchy while keeping the flow compact. The breadcrumb never reaches the right edge of the card, leaving ample blank space to the right that reinforces the editorial, content-first aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline at the top of the main content card; moves naturally with scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The program-info-panel occupies the full width of the content card beneath the breadcrumb. At its top, the program title is set as a large, bold serif heading in the museum’s red, left-aligned and spanning roughly 70–80% of the card’s width, leaving a comfortable right margin. Directly below the title, any subtitle or brief metadata (duration, location) appears in a slightly smaller, muted serif line. Following that, one or more paragraphs of descriptive text run full width within the card’s inner padding, with 24px spacing between heading, subheading, and paragraphs. Inline emphasis such as links or small icons are used sparingly, echoing the mockup’s understated use of color within text. Overall, this panel fills roughly the upper third to upper two-fifths of the card’s height, establishing the program’s context before any booking controls appear.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width block within the content card; expands vertically with longer descriptions, pushing all subsequent elements downward in the linear flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The session-availability-indicator is visually tied to the program-info-panel but occupies a compact area near the top-right portion of that section. It appears either as a small pill or label (e.g., 'Available', 'Fully booked') aligned to the right edge of the card’s inner padding, vertically aligned with or slightly below the baseline of the main program title. Its width is modest—about 10–20% of the card width at most—and its height similar to a single line of text plus 4–8px vertical padding. This placement lets users notice availability with a quick glance after reading the title, without interrupting the left-aligned narrative text. If additional indicators (e.g., next available date) are shown, they stack in a short vertical cluster aligned to the right, still confined to the same heading band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, positioned within the same vertical band as the program title; floats to the right within that band but remains within the card’s padding boundaries and scrolls normally.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The session-list-selector sits directly below the program-info-panel’s descriptive text, separated by a 24–32px vertical gap. It spans the full usable width of the card and is structured as a horizontal grid of session cards, echoing the mockup’s four-column image row. Each session card behaves like one column in a four-column internal grid: on wide viewports, up to four cards appear per row, each occupying roughly 22–24% of the card width, with 8–12px gutters between them. The cards have thin, light grey borders and a slightly off-white background, giving them the feel of small archival tiles. Each card includes a date/time heading at the top, compact details below, and a subtle state indicator (e.g., 'Few spots left') near the bottom, all stacked with 4–8px vertical spacing. Multiple rows of cards stack vertically with 16–24px gaps between rows if needed. A small label like 'Choose a session' can sit left-aligned above the first row, keeping consistency with the editorial style. This band visually occupies the middle portion of the card, about 25–35% of its height, and acts as a strong horizontal scanning zone.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static grid within the card; wraps session cards to additional rows as needed and reflows responsively while preserving the centered, card-width grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The participant-selection-form appears below the session-list-selector with a clear 24–32px vertical separation. It returns to a predominantly single-column arrangement consistent with the editorial layout. A simple section label such as 'Participants' or 'Who is joining?' is left-aligned at the top of this block. Below it, form fields (e.g., number of adults, children, concessions) are arranged in one or two horizontal lines: labels on the left, numeric inputs or dropdowns on the right, each pair spanning roughly 40–60% of the card width to avoid overly long lines. Vertically, each field row is separated by 8–16px, maintaining clarity without excessive whitespace. The entire form block spans the full width of the card but visually feels more compact than the narrative text block, occupying roughly 15–20% of the card’s height. Error messages for individual fields appear directly beneath the relevant field in small, red text, indented to align with the input.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width form section within the content card; expands vertically as fields or helper texts increase, pushing the price summary and button downward.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The price-summary-panel sits immediately below the participant-selection-form, separated by about 24px of vertical space. It is visually framed as a compact summary block spanning the full width of the card but visually weighted toward the right. The panel uses a very light tinted background or a subtle top border to distinguish it from the rest of the card while remaining flat and simple. Within this block, a short list of line items (e.g., 'Adults × 2', 'Children × 1') is left-aligned with small vertical spacing (4–8px), and the total price is right-aligned at the bottom in a slightly larger, bolder serif style. On wider screens, the panel content is horizontally organized: description on the left half and total on the right half, all within the same panel. Vertically, the summary occupies about 10–15% of the card height, leaving enough room below for the primary-action-button while keeping the bottom of the card visually anchored.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width informational block situated just above the primary action; resizes vertically with content but does not detach or stick during scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-validation-controller manifests as a slim message area just above the price-summary-panel and primary-action-button zone. When there are validation issues (e.g., no session selected, missing participant count), a horizontally full-width banner appears within the card, spanning from left to right padding edges and occupying the height of one to two lines of text plus 8–12px padding. The banner uses a light background tint (soft red or amber) with left-aligned text in a small serif style, and may include a simple icon at the far left. It sits about 8–16px above the price-summary-panel, giving the impression of a note pinned just above the booking summary. When no validation issues exist, this area collapses entirely, preserving the clean vertical rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered inline block; appears and disappears without shifting overall page structure beyond its own small vertical height, and scrolls with the rest of the content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-submit-controller occupies the same horizontal region as the primary-action-button and price-summary-panel at the bottom of the card. When a booking submission is in progress, it slightly dims the contents of the price summary and primary-action-button (e.g., via lowered opacity) and overlays a small, centrally positioned spinner or 'Processing your booking…' label within the lower portion of the card. This overlay is centered horizontally within the card and vertically aligned near the button area, covering roughly the bottom 20–25% of the card’s height while leaving the rest of the content still visible but visually deprioritized. The effect is that of a subtle glass pane over the booking controls, indicating that the user should wait.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Transient overlay anchored to the lower section of the content card; positioned above the price summary and button in stacking order, and scrolls with the card content if the user has scrolled down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The primary-action-button is the culminating element of the card, positioned at the bottom-right of the content card’s inner padding, directly beneath or aligned with the price-summary-panel. Horizontally, it sits flush with the right padding, with a small gap (8–16px) from the right edge of the price summary text if they are on the same row. Vertically, there is 16–24px of spacing between the bottom of the price-summary-panel and the top edge of the button. The button is medium-sized, with 8–10px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding, rendered in the brand’s strong red with white text and a simple rectangular shape with slightly softened corners. It occupies roughly 15–20% of the card width, leaving empty space to the left for balance. This placement makes the button a clear endpoint in the vertical flow without overwhelming the conservative design.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static call-to-action anchored to the bottom-right within the card; remains in place relative to other content and is not sticky, requiring a natural scroll to reach.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The load-error-state-panel replaces the normal card content when data cannot be loaded. The same centered white card container is shown beneath the global header, with the panel’s content vertically and horizontally centered within it. The error message appears as a short heading in muted red or dark grey, left-aligned or center-aligned within a narrow column in the middle of the card. Below the message, an optional retry button appears in a subdued style, centered within the same column. The entire message area occupies the central 40–50% of the card’s height, framed by generous whitespace at the top and bottom to keep the tone calm and consistent with the museum’s understated aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Replaces the usual content inside the centered card; static and scrolls with the page, with no overlay behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-state-manager does not present a visible UI element but determines which of the loader, error, or main content card is displayed in the central content region beneath the global header. Conceptually, it controls a single vertical slot in the layout: this slot always holds a centered white card spanning 70–80% of the viewport width, but the inner content of that card changes according to state (loading, error, or full program detail and booking flow). From the user’s perspective, the card itself is stable in position and size; only its internal content shifts, preserving a consistent spatial anchor on the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual controller that swaps which child layout (loader, error panel, or full detail layout) occupies the main card region; it does not consume its own visible space beyond that shared content area.

name: Booking Review
title: Booking Review
description: Review the details of a selected tour or workshop booking, then confirm it and optionally add it to the cart.

core_features: Read the booking_id from the URL and load the booking review data using getProgramBookingReview.
Display a summary of the selected program (title, type, language, duration) and its specific session (date, start time, optional end time).
Show participant counts (adult, child, senior) and the calculated total price with currency.
Provide a link back to the program detail page to change the session or adjust participant numbers.
Allow the user to confirm the pending booking via finalizeProgramBooking with two options: confirm only, or confirm and add to cart.
Redirect the user to cart.html after a successful confirmation with add_to_cart = true.
Offer navigation links back to the tours & workshops listing and to the program detail page.
Handle missing or invalid booking_id and non-pending booking statuses with clear error or status messages and alternative navigation options.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Review booking after selection on Program Detail:
1. User completes the booking form on program_detail.html and is redirected to booking_review.html with a booking_id in the URL.
2. On page load, the page reads booking_id from the URL query string.
3. The page calls getProgramBookingReview(booking_id).
4. If the call succeeds, the booking summary, participant counts, session details, and total price are displayed.
5. User reviews the information; if changes are needed, they click the 'Change session or participants' link, which navigates to program_detail.html?id=booking.program_id.
6. On program_detail.html, the user adjusts session/participants and submits again, which creates or updates a pending ProgramBooking and redirects back to booking_review.html with a (possibly new) booking_id.
7. The Booking Review page reloads and repeats steps 2–4 with the updated booking.

Workflow 2 – Confirm booking and add to cart:
1. User is satisfied with the booking details on booking_review.html.
2. User clicks the 'Confirm and add to cart' action.
3. The page calls finalizeProgramBooking(booking_id, add_to_cart = true) and disables the action buttons while the request is in progress.
4. If success is true, the returned booking is used to update the local view state (status now confirmed), and cart_item is stored in local page state for display if needed.
5. The user is redirected to cart.html so they can review their full cart.
6. If finalizeProgramBooking returns an error or success is false, an error message is shown and the action buttons are re-enabled.

Workflow 3 – Confirm booking without viewing cart immediately:
1. User is satisfied with the booking details on booking_review.html but wants to keep browsing tours before viewing the cart.
2. User clicks the 'Confirm booking' action (without add to cart redirect).
3. The page calls finalizeProgramBooking(booking_id, add_to_cart = false) and disables the action buttons while the request is in progress.
4. If success is true, the booking status in the view is updated to confirmed.
5. A confirmation message is displayed; primary navigation options include a link to tours_workshops.html?view=guided and an optional link to cart.html.
6. If the call fails, an error message appears and the buttons are re-enabled.

Workflow 4 – Direct reload via URL:
1. User or agent manually navigates to booking_review.html?booking_id={booking_id}.
2. The page reads booking_id from the URL and calls getProgramBookingReview(booking_id).
3. If the booking exists and is pending, the normal review view is shown with confirmation actions.
4. If the booking has status confirmed, the page shows a read-only confirmation summary and hides confirmation actions, offering links to tours_workshops.html and cart.html instead.
5. If the booking is cancelled or not found, the page shows an appropriate error or information message and a link back to tours_workshops.html?view=guided.

Workflow 5 – Handling missing or invalid booking_id:
1. User reaches booking_review.html without a booking_id parameter, or with an invalid value (e.g., empty string).
2. The page detects that booking_id is missing/invalid and does not call getProgramBookingReview.
3. An error message is shown indicating that the booking cannot be found.
4. The user is offered navigation options to go back to tours_workshops.html?view=guided or to another appropriate starting page.
interactions:
On page load (initialization):
- Trigger: Page is loaded in the browser.
- Behavior: Read booking_id from the URL query string. If present, call getProgramBookingReview(booking_id). Show a loading state until the request resolves. If missing, show an error state and do not call the interface.
- Result: On success, the page state is populated with booking (ProgramBooking), program (Program), and session (ProgramSession) data; on failure, an error message and navigation options are displayed.

Click 'Change session or participants':
- Trigger: User clicks the change link in the booking summary area.
- Behavior: Navigate via standard link to program_detail.html?id=booking.program_id.
- Result: The user can adjust session or participant counts on the program detail page; after they submit, that page redirects back to booking_review.html with an updated booking_id.

Click 'View program details' (if provided):
- Trigger: User clicks a program title or explicit 'View program details' link.
- Behavior: Navigate to program_detail.html?id=booking.program_id.
- Result: The user can read full program information and optionally start a new booking flow.

Click 'Confirm and add to cart':
- Trigger: User clicks the main confirmation button when they want to finalize the booking and go to the cart.
- Behavior: Call finalizeProgramBooking(booking_id, add_to_cart = true). Disable confirmation buttons and show a loading state during the call. Prevent multiple submissions.
- Result on success: The returned booking object updates the page state (showing status confirmed). The returned cart_item object is stored in-page if needed for display. The user is then redirected to cart.html.
- Result on failure: Show an error notification. The confirmation buttons are re-enabled and no navigation occurs.

Click 'Confirm booking' (without immediate cart):
- Trigger: User clicks an alternative confirmation button for finalizing the booking but wants to stay in the content area.
- Behavior: Call finalizeProgramBooking(booking_id, add_to_cart = false). Disable confirmation buttons and show a loading state.
- Result on success: Booking status is updated to confirmed in the view. Confirmation actions are hidden or disabled. A success message is shown with links to tours_workshops.html?view=guided and cart.html.
- Result on failure: Show an error notification and re-enable the buttons.

Click 'Back to Tours' or 'Browse more tours':
- Trigger: User clicks a navigation link in the action or navigation area.
- Behavior: Navigate to tours_workshops.html?view=guided using a standard link.
- Result: User returns to the tours & workshops list to browse or start another booking.

Click 'Go to Cart' (after non-cart confirmation):
- Trigger: After confirming without add_to_cart redirect, user clicks 'Go to Cart' in a success message or navigation area.
- Behavior: Navigate directly to cart.html via standard link. (Cart contents are assumed to be managed globally, including any cart_item added by other flows.)
- Result: User reviews their entire cart.

Handling non-pending booking states:
- Trigger: getProgramBookingReview returns a booking whose status is not 'pending' (e.g., 'confirmed' or 'cancelled').
- Behavior: The page displays a read-only summary and an informational status message. Confirmation buttons are not shown or are disabled with an explanation.
- Result: User understands they cannot re-confirm this booking from this page and can navigate using the provided links.

Error handling for data loading and confirmation:
- Trigger: getProgramBookingReview or finalizeProgramBooking returns an error or fails.
- Behavior: Display an error notification with generic failure text, and show navigation options to tours_workshops.html?view=guided. When appropriate, allow the user to retry the action (reload booking, resubmit finalize). Buttons reflect disabled/enabled states based on whether a retry is in progress.
- Result: User is informed about the failure and has a clear next step.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state driver for this page is the booking_id URL parameter. On every page load, the script reads booking_id from location.search. If booking_id is present and non-empty, the page calls getProgramBookingReview(booking_id) to retrieve the booking context. If booking_id is absent or empty, the page enters an error state and does not call any interface. The page does not rely on localStorage; when the user returns from program_detail.html after editing their booking, that page is responsible for redirecting back to booking_review.html with the (possibly new) booking_id in the query string, which causes a fresh getProgramBookingReview call. The internal page state stores the latest response objects (booking, program, session) for rendering and interaction logic. The booking.status field determines whether confirmation actions are available: if booking.status is 'pending', the confirm buttons are enabled; if it is 'confirmed' or 'cancelled', the page shows a read-only view and hides or disables confirming actions. The page does not update the URL after confirmation; status changes are reflected in the in-memory state based on the booking object returned from finalizeProgramBooking. Navigation links use URL parameters in a predictable way: the 'Change session or participants' link always goes to program_detail.html?id=booking.program_id, and the 'Back to Tours' link always goes to tours_workshops.html?view=guided. The Cart page is reached via a static cart.html URL, relying on global cart state maintained outside this page.

components.functionality: Initializes the Booking Review page by reading the booking_id from the URL, calling getProgramBookingReview, managing loading and error states, and providing booking, program, and session data to other components.

Displays the main booking summary including program title, program_type, language, duration, session date and time, and booking status. Provides navigation to the program detail page for viewing full information or changing session/participants.

Shows the participant breakdown (adult_count, child_count, senior_count) from the booking and the total_price with currency. Optionally includes a derived total participants count for display. Provides a secondary link to adjust participants via the program detail page.

Provides actions to finalize the booking: confirm and add to cart, or confirm without immediate cart redirect. Calls finalizeProgramBooking with appropriate add_to_cart parameter, manages in-progress state, and updates local booking status based on the response.

Displays informational or error messages related to the booking state (pending, confirmed, cancelled), loading failures, and finalize errors. Reacts to changes in booking.status and the results of getProgramBookingReview and finalizeProgramBooking.

Provides contextual navigation links from the Booking Review page, including 'Back to Tours' (tours_workshops.html?view=guided), 'Go to Cart' (cart.html), and 'Change session or participants' (program_detail.html?id=booking.program_id). Links are enabled or disabled based on booking status when appropriate.

Handles cases where booking_id is missing from the URL or where getProgramBookingReview fails to return a valid booking. Displays a concise explanation and navigation options to tours_workshops.html?view=guided.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width grid similar to a 12-column system with a main content column and an internal 4-column image grid. Each image/caption acts like one column of four within the content width.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a single, centered main content card beneath the global museum header, mirroring the editorial, content-first layout. Inside the card, content is arranged in a linear top-to-bottom flow: page title and explanatory text (part of the page shell), then any booking status/messages, followed by the booking summary panel, then the participant and price summary, then the booking confirmation actions, and finally the navigation links at the very bottom. The loader and error fallback temporarily take over the card's inner area as needed, but respect the same centered, single-column structure.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a single, centered main content card beneath the global museum header, mirroring the editorial, content-first layout. Inside the card, content is arranged in a linear top-to-bottom flow: page title and explanatory text (part of the page shell), then any booking status/messages, followed by the booking summary panel, then the participant and price summary, then the booking confirmation actions, and finally the navigation links at the very bottom. The loader and error fallback temporarily take over the card's inner area as needed, but respect the same centered, single-column structure.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content card sits centered in the viewport, spanning roughly 70–80% of the page width, with soft grey page background visible on both sides. The top global header occupies about 15–20% of the viewport height, with the card starting below it and dominating the remaining vertical space. Within the card, the informational panels (booking summary + participant and price summary) occupy the upper ~60% of the card's height, while the action panel and navigation links form a tighter band in the lower ~40%. Vertical spacing between these major blocks follows the 24–32px rhythm, with the card itself padded by roughly 24–32px on all sides.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is moderately dense and text-first: lines of text span a comfortable width (roughly 60–70 characters), with 1.4–1.6 line height. Panels are distinct but not overly separated: gaps between panels are in the 24–32px range, while internal padding within panels sits around 16–24px. The layout avoids excessive whitespace but keeps enough breathing room for clarity, echoing the traditional, trustworthy editorial tone. Buttons and links are compact yet readable, using 8–12px horizontal and 4–8px vertical padding.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the red brand bar and logo in the global header, then moves to the centered card’s red page title and introductory copy. From there, attention drops to any status/message notification band, then to the booking summary panel, followed directly by the participant and price summary. The eye is then naturally drawn to the strong red primary action in the booking confirmation actions panel, and finally down to the subtle, text-only navigation links at the bottom. When present, the loader or error fallback temporarily become the focal point by occupying the central area of the card, but they still align within this vertical flow.

layout.overall_description: Below the museum’s red-topped global header, a single white content card sits centered on a light grey background, spanning about three quarters of the viewport width. The card has generous 24–32px padding and a subtle shadow or border, giving it a formal editorial feel. At its top (outside the scope of the listed components) sit the page title “Booking Review” in a red serif and a short explanatory paragraph. Immediately below, a full-width notification band appears when there are booking status messages, followed by two stacked, lightly framed information panels: the first summarizing the booked tour or workshop, the second detailing participants and pricing. These two panels occupy most of the upper portion of the card, creating a structured narrative of what has been booked. Beneath them, a horizontal strip of actions features a prominent red confirmation button and any secondary actions, anchored to the left yet surrounded by comfortable whitespace. At the bottom edge of the card, low-emphasis text navigation links allow the user to go back or move to related flows. When the booking data is loading, a pale overlay within the card centers a spinner and loading text, dimming but not hiding the card frame. On errors, the core of the card is taken over by a single, centered error block, while still respecting the same margins and typographic hierarchy. Overall, the page feels like a carefully typeset museum brochure translated into web form: orderly, content-first, and easy to scan top-to-bottom.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-review-init-loader appears inside the bounds of the main content card, overlaying all inner content while keeping the card’s outer frame and title visible. It covers the full inner width of the card (the same 70–80% of viewport width) and extends from just below the page title and intro paragraph down to the bottom padding of the card. The overlay uses a semi-opaque white or very light grey background so underlying structure is hinted at but not interactive. At the vertical and horizontal center of this overlay sits a small spinner icon (or loading indicator) with a single line of descriptive text directly underneath, both center-aligned. The center content occupies roughly 20–25% of the card width, with at least 64px of empty space around it on all sides, maintaining the calm, museum-like feel. The top of the overlay is separated from the card’s top padding by about 16–24px, ensuring it reads as part of the card rather than a global page loader.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as an absolute-positioned overlay within the main content card: fixed relative to the card’s inner edges, spanning full card width and height. It temporarily blocks interaction with all underlying components and scrolls away with the card as a single block if the page scrolls. Once data is loaded, it fades out or is removed, allowing the standard stacked components beneath to become visible and interactive.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-summary-panel is positioned near the top of the main content card’s body, directly beneath any booking status notification (if present) and a 24–32px gap below the explanatory paragraph. It spans the full usable width inside the card’s padding, aligning left and right with the page title above. The panel itself is a rectangular block with a thin grey border or very subtle shadow and an off-white or slightly tinted background to distinguish it from the pure white card. It uses 16–24px of internal padding on all sides. Vertically, this panel takes up roughly the upper third of the card’s content area when all components are present, leaving room for the participant/price panel and actions below. Inside, the content can follow a two-column feel at larger breakpoints: primary tour/workshop details (title, date, time, location) aligned to the left two-thirds, and a compact set of key facts (duration, language, category) aligned to the right third, while remaining stacked and fully responsive on narrow screens. The bottom of this panel ends with a clean baseline that aligns horizontally with the start of the next panel, separated by about 24px of white space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, block-level element within the card’s linear flow. It expands vertically based on its content, always maintaining the same full-width alignment and inter-panel spacing, and shifts down as needed if status messages or other components appear above it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The participant-and-price-summary sits immediately below the booking-summary-panel, separated by a consistent 24px vertical gap that visually groups the two as related but distinct. It also spans the full inner width of the card and aligns perfectly with the edges of the booking summary panel, reinforcing the single-column editorial grid. The panel has a similar structural treatment—a thin grey border or subtle outline and a light, neutral background—but may use slightly less visual weight (e.g., lighter border or less contrast) to let the booking summary remain the narrative starting point. Internal padding of 16–24px ensures legibility. Inside the panel, content organizes into a left area listing participant types and counts (e.g., adults, children) and a right-aligned column summarizing price subtotals and a bold total at the bottom. On wider screens, the left and right areas behave like two equal or 60/40 columns within the panel; on narrow screens they stack vertically with a 16px gap. The panel’s height flexes with content but typically occupies another third or so of the card’s inner height, leading naturally into the action area below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, block-level element that follows directly after the booking summary in the vertical flow. It remains attached to that summary panel visually via consistent alignment and spacing and shifts up or down depending on the presence of notifications above.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-confirmation-actions panel is positioned below the participant-and-price-summary, with a slightly larger gap—approximately 32px—to signal a transition from information to decision-making. It occupies the full inner width of the card but has a low structural footprint: rather than a framed box, this area is mostly whitespace with left-aligned buttons and textual hints. The primary action button, styled in the brand’s strong red, sits at the top-left of this area, directly under the preceding panel’s left edge. The button is sized to feel substantial but not oversized, with around 10–12px horizontal and 6–8px vertical padding, and does not stretch the full width; instead it occupies about 20–30% of the card width on desktop. To its immediate right, at a distance of 16–24px, sits an optional secondary button or link-style action in a more neutral style (outline or text-only), ensuring the red primary remains the focal point. Below the buttons, aligned to the left and separated by roughly 8–12px of vertical space, a short line of helper text or small-print note can appear, using smaller type and a muted color. Overall, this actions band takes up the lower quarter or so of the card’s body, with ample white space above and below to echo the calm, editorial aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, block-level section that remains anchored to the main card’s flow. It does not stick to the viewport; instead, it scrolls naturally with the page. The buttons wrap or stack vertically on narrow screens, maintaining left alignment and consistent vertical spacing in line with the spacing system.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-status-and-messages component occupies a horizontal band near the top of the main content card’s body, just below the page heading and introductory paragraph and above the booking-summary-panel. It spans the full usable width of the card, aligning with all other panels. The band has a lightly tinted background (e.g., pale green for success, amber for warning, light red for errors) and a thin border in a slightly stronger version of that color, setting it apart while still fitting the restrained palette. Inside, a small status icon sits on the far left, followed by message text aligned to the left margin with around 16px padding between icon and text. Internal padding of about 12–16px on all sides keeps the message compact but readable. The entire band is separated from the intro text above and the booking-summary-panel below by approximately 16–24px, so it reads clearly as a notice without overpowering the main narrative. When no messages exist, this band collapses entirely, allowing the booking-summary-panel to move up and occupy the space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static notification strip within the card’s vertical flow. It appears or disappears dynamically based on status, causing subsequent components to shift up or down but never overlaying content. It does not stick or float; it scrolls away with the rest of the card content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-navigation-links sit at the very bottom of the main content card, following the booking-confirmation-actions area with about 24–32px of vertical space. The links occupy a single horizontal row that spans the card width, but the content remains low-profile and text-based to preserve the hierarchy. On larger screens, a primary back link (e.g., “Back to date selection”) is aligned to the left edge of the content column, while a secondary forward or alternative path link (e.g., “Go to cart”) can be right-aligned on the same baseline, creating a subtle left-right tension. The text is set in a smaller, regular-weight type with a muted color (dark grey) and underlines or subtle color shifts on hover, aligning with the conservative editorial style. The area itself has minimal extra padding, relying on the card’s base padding and the 16–24px vertical gap from the actions above. On narrow screens, the links stack vertically with an 8–12px gap between them, both left-aligned. The card’s bottom padding of about 24–32px sits just below these links, giving them a modest buffer before the page background.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, anchored to the bottom of the card’s content stack. It scrolls with the card and does not fix to the viewport. If the page content is short, it may appear near the middle of the viewport; if the content is long, it will be reached after scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-error-fallback takes over the main content region of the card when a critical error prevents the booking details from loading. In this state, the usual inner components (status messages, summary panels, actions, navigation links) are hidden or suppressed, and instead a single, centered error block is displayed beneath the page title and any brief explanation. The error block is horizontally centered within the card and capped at roughly 60% of the card’s width, creating generous margins on both sides. Vertically, it is placed in the middle third of the card—leaving at least 64px of breathing room above and below—to avoid feeling cramped. At the top of this block, a small, simple icon (e.g., an outlined warning symbol) sits centered, followed by a bold error title on the next line, then 1–2 lines of explanatory text in regular weight, all center-aligned. Below the text, a single prominent action (such as “Try again”) appears as a red button, with an optional secondary text link (e.g., “Return to overview”) beneath it in muted styling. Internal spacing follows the 8–16px increments between icon, headings, text, and button, giving the block a composed, poster-like quality within the card’s frame.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditional, static replacement for the normal stacked content. When active, it occupies the same general card area, pushes nothing else below it (as other components are hidden), and scrolls with the page like any other block. It does not overlay the global header or card frame; instead, it respects the same card padding and central alignment as the standard content.

name: Learn & Articles
title: Learn & Articles
description: Provides access to learning resources about cheese history, with an integrated articles list that can be filtered by topic, time period, and reading time, and links to detailed articles and the user's reading list.

core_features: Load and display introductory Learn page content, featured topics, and highlighted articles using the learn page content interface.
Provide an 'Articles' section entry point (button or header control) that moves focus/scroll to the articles list area.
Expose filter controls for articles (topic, time period, reading time) using options from the article filter options interface.
Initialize article filters from URL parameters when present, so that navigation from other pages can preconfigure the article list.
List articles based on the active filters, with pagination and optional sorting, using the article listing interface.
Allow navigation to individual article detail pages when users click an article in the list.
Provide a link to the user's 'My Reading List' page, accessible from the Learn page.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Initial page load with or without URL parameters
1) User opens the Learn & Articles page via navigation or a direct link.
2) The page controller parses URL parameters for topic, start_year, end_year, and reading_time.
3) The page calls the learn page content interface to retrieve introductory text, featured topics, and highlighted articles.
4) The page calls the article filter options interface to obtain available topic options, time period presets, and reading time options.
5) The filter components initialize their state based on URL parameters where applicable, falling back to default states if no parameters are present.
6) The page calls the article listing interface with the derived filter set, initial sort option, and initial page index.
7) The article list displays results; the scroll position highlights or focuses the general Learn content, with the articles section available further down the page.

Workflow: Jumping directly to the articles section
1) User clicks the 'Articles' section button or header control at the top of the Learn page.
2) The page scrolls or moves focus to the articles list container without changing filters or URL parameters.
3) The user now sees the current filtered list of articles and can refine filters or navigate to articles.

Workflow: Filtering articles by topic, time period, and reading time
1) User views the filter bar above the article list, which is populated with options from the article filter options interface.
2) The user selects or changes a topic filter option.
3) The topic filter component updates internal filter state and triggers a unified filter change event.
4) The page controller updates the URL query string to reflect the new topic parameter while preserving existing time period, reading time, and pagination parameters as appropriate.
5) The page controller resets the current page to the first page and calls the article listing interface with the updated filters.
6) The article list updates to show articles matching the new topic.
7) Similarly, when the user selects a time period preset or adjusts a custom start/end year, or chooses a reading time option, the filter state is updated, the URL is synchronized, and the article listing interface is called again with the new filters.
8) The updated article list is rendered according to the combined active filters.

Workflow: Navigating through paginated article results
1) User scrolls the articles list to the bottom where pagination controls are shown.
2) The user selects a different page number or uses next/previous controls.
3) The pagination component triggers a page change event with the new page index.
4) The page controller updates the URL query string with the new page parameter while preserving current filter parameters.
5) The article listing interface is called with the same filters but the new page index and page size.
6) The article list component replaces its content with articles returned for the requested page and scrolls to the top of the article list area if needed.

Workflow: Opening an article detail from the Learn page
1) User scans the article list and decides to open a specific article.
2) The user clicks the article title or a dedicated "Read more" control within the article list item.
3) The article list item component uses the article identifier from the listing result to construct a navigation URL to the article detail page, passing the article id as a URL parameter.
4) The browser navigates to the article detail page, where more detailed content is shown (handled outside this page).

Workflow: Accessing 'My Reading List' from Learn & Articles
1) User notices the 'My Reading List' link in the Learn & Articles page.
2) The user clicks the link.
3) The browser navigates to the reading list page using a standard link, without requiring additional interfaces from this page.
4) Any reading list management occurs on the target page; the Learn page does not modify reading list data directly.
interactions:
Interaction: Page initialization
- Trigger: Page load event.
- Behavior: Parse URL parameters; call learn page content interface and article filter options interface; derive initial filter state from URL; call article listing interface using the derived filters, sort option, and page index; render intro content, featured topics, highlighted articles, filter controls, and initial article list.

Interaction: Clicking a featured topic in the Learn intro section
- Trigger: User clicks a featured topic chip or card in the intro area.
- Behavior: The featured topic component emits a topic selection event; the filter bar updates the topic filter state to the selected topic; the page controller updates the topic parameter in the URL and resets pagination; the article listing interface is called with the updated filters; the view scrolls to the articles section so the user immediately sees relevant articles.

Interaction: Clicking the 'Articles' button or header
- Trigger: User clicks the 'Articles' section button or header control.
- Behavior: The page scrolls or moves focus to the articles list container; no API calls or filter changes occur.

Interaction: Changing the topic filter from the filters bar
- Trigger: User selects a topic from the topic filter control.
- Behavior: The topic filter component updates its selected value; the page controller merges this value into the current filters; the URL query string is updated for the topic parameter and the page parameter is reset; the article listing interface is called with the updated filters; the article list updates.

Interaction: Selecting a time period preset
- Trigger: User clicks a time period preset option.
- Behavior: The time period filter component sets both internal start and end year values based on the selected preset; the page controller updates the corresponding URL parameters; pagination is reset; the article listing interface is called with the updated time period filter; the article list updates.

Interaction: Adjusting a custom time period range
- Trigger: User edits custom start and/or end year fields and applies the change.
- Behavior: The time period filter component validates and emits the new start and end values; the page controller updates the URL parameters for years; pagination is reset; the article listing interface is called with the new custom range; the article list updates.

Interaction: Selecting a reading time option
- Trigger: User chooses a reading time option from the reading time control.
- Behavior: The reading time filter component updates its selected value; the page controller updates the corresponding URL parameter; pagination is reset; the article listing interface is called with the updated filters; the article list component refreshes.

Interaction: Changing sort order for articles (if sorting is exposed)
- Trigger: User selects a different sort option (for example by date or reading time) in a sort control.
- Behavior: The sort control updates the selected sort_by value; the page controller updates this value in internal state and optionally in the URL; pagination is reset; the article listing interface is called with the same filters but new sort_by; the list updates in the chosen order.

Interaction: Navigating pagination controls
- Trigger: User clicks a pagination control (specific page, next, or previous).
- Behavior: The pagination component emits the target page index; the page controller updates the page parameter in the URL; the article listing interface is called with the same filters and new page index; the list updates; focus may be moved to the top of the list for accessibility.

Interaction: Opening an article from the list
- Trigger: User clicks on an article list item title or a dedicated "Read more" action.
- Behavior: The article list item uses its bound article id to build the destination URL for the article detail page; the browser navigates to that URL, passing the article id as a parameter.

Interaction: Navigating to 'My Reading List'
- Trigger: User clicks the 'My Reading List' link.
